grounding_note: Expansion batch: more seller-identity and transaction-structure disclosures.

scenarios.opening_query: The van we rely on for my wheelchair broke down for the fourth time since we bought it in the spring.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || Consumer Law > Problems Between Consumers
scenarios.hidden_fact: We bought it from a retired couple at their house — a private sale with a handwritten bill of sale.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We bought it from a retired couple at their home — private sale, handwritten bill of sale.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the seller was a dealer or private party
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It came from an accessibility-vehicle dealership with a 90-day powertrain warranty they now dodge.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It came from an accessibility-vehicle dealer with a 90-day warranty they're dodging.
scenarios.vetting_note: Same reliance and hardship; seller identity routes it.

scenarios.opening_query: The dealership added $4,000 in 'protection products' I explicitly declined, and it's buried in the contract.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || Consumer Law > Banking
scenarios.hidden_fact: The finance company owns the paper now and refuses to remove the products — and I've found they fund this exact add-on scheme across many dealers; the lender's practice is the target.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The finance company owns the loan and won't strip the products — and they fund this same add-on scheme across lots of dealers. They're the target.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the lender's financing practice rather than the single sale is the claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The lender's fine — they told me the dealer must correct it, and the dealer refuses. It's the dealership's fraud.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The lender says the dealer must fix it and the dealer refuses — it's the dealership's fraud.
scenarios.vetting_note: Dealer-vs-financier defendant selection behind one packed contract.

scenarios.opening_query: I sold my motorcycle to a guy who now claims I hid problems with it. He wants double his money back.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Problems Between Consumers || Consumer Law > Small Claims Advice
scenarios.hidden_fact: He filed in small claims — hearing in a month, $4,800 demanded — and I need help preparing my defense myself.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He filed in small claims — hearing in a month, asking $4,800 — and I want help preparing my own defense.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a filed small-claims case is the live posture
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing's filed — it's demand letters and one-star reviews so far.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing filed — just demand letters and bad reviews so far.
scenarios.vetting_note: Seller-side stated role; filing posture is the fact.

scenarios.opening_query: The used SUV we financed turned out to have an open recall the seller never mentioned and now it stalls in traffic.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || General Litigation > Products Liability
scenarios.hidden_fact: It stalled on the highway and we were rear-ended — my son's collarbone broke, so this is about the defect injuring him, not just the sale.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It stalled on the highway and we got rear-ended — my son's collarbone broke. It's about the defect hurting him now.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the defect caused a physical injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No one's been hurt — we want out of the deal and the loan over the concealed recall.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nobody's hurt — we want out of the deal and loan over the hidden recall.
scenarios.vetting_note: Injury-from-defect converts sale dispute to products liability.

scenarios.opening_query: A car I co-signed for my nephew is now a repossession mess landing on my credit.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || Debtor/Creditor > General (Debtor)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The car's gone and sold — what's chasing me is an $11,000 deficiency demand and a collection lawsuit naming me as co-signer.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The car's sold — I'm facing an $11,000 deficiency demand and a collection suit as co-signer.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the dispute is post-repossession deficiency collection
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The repo itself was wrongful — payments were current under the modification the lender itself approved, and I want the repo attacked.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The repo was wrongful — payments were current under the lender's own modification.
scenarios.vetting_note: Deficiency-defense vs wrongful-repo; posture fact decides.

scenarios.opening_query: We bought a camper trailer that leaked from day one, and the seller's 'warranty' turned out to be worthless.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Automobiles/RV's/Mobile Homes || Consumer Law > Insurance
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'warranty' is a third-party service contract from a company that denies every claim as 'pre-existing' — the state has dozens of complaints; the contract seller is the target.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The 'warranty' is a third-party service contract that denies everything as 'pre-existing' — the state has dozens of complaints on them.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a third-party service-contract company's practices are the claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The warranty is the dealer's own — and they simply refuse to honor it on the leak.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the dealer's own warranty — they just won't honor it.
scenarios.vetting_note: Service-contract-company practices route to insurance-adjacent consumer law.
